invoice.raw_text:
{
  "invoice_number": "INV-1004",
  "revision": "R1",
  "vendor": {
    "name": "Precision Parts Ltd.",
    "address": "742 Evergreen Terrace, Springfield, IL 62704"
  },
  "date": "2026-01-22",
  "due_date": "2026-02-22",
  "line_items": [
    {"item": "WidgetA", "quantity": 3, "unit_price": 250.00},
    {"item": "WidgetB", "quantity": 2, "unit_price": 500.00},
    {"item": "GadgetX", "quantity": 5, "unit_price": 750.00}
  ],
  "subtotal": 5500.00,
  "tax_rate": 0.08,
  "tax_amount": 440.00,
  "total": 5940.00,
  "currency": "USD",
  "payment_terms": "Net 30",
  "notes": "Revised invoice - additional items added per PO amendment"
}

decision.rationale: After considering the critique, I acknowledge the validity of the raised concerns regarding the future invoice date (2026-01-22) and the revision note in the raw text about additional items per PO amendment. These are potential red flags that warrant further scrutiny for error or fraud, despite the auto_approve flag being true. A manual review is necessary to verify the date and ensure the revisions align with the original purchase order.
